channel.title: NPR Topics: Economy
channel.description: NPR news on the U.S. and world economy, the World Bank, and Federal Reserve. Commentary on economic trends. Subscribe to NPR Economy podcasts and RSS feeds.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: June gloom for the job market as employers add just 57,000 jobs
item.description: U.S. employers added fewer jobs than expected last month. The unemployment rate fell, but only because a large number of people stopped looking for work.
item.content:encoded: U.S. employers added fewer jobs than expected last month. The unemployment rate fell, but only because a large number of people stopped looking for work.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: U.S. job market slows in June
item.description: Employers added 57,000 jobs in June, the Labor Department said on Friday, as jobs growth slowed from the previous two months, while the unemployment rate ticked down to 4.2%.
item.content:encoded: Employers added 57,000 jobs in June, the Labor Department said on Friday, as jobs growth slowed from the previous two months, while the unemployment rate ticked down to 4.2%.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: If wars are so costly, why do we keep fighting them?
item.description: We dig into current conflicts to understand the incentives that lead countries into violence, and what keeps them stretching on and on.
item.content:encoded: We dig into current conflicts to understand the incentives that lead countries into violence, and what keeps them stretching on and on.
item.dc:creator: Darian Woods

item.title: Could high inflation be the new normal?
item.description: Inflation is at a three-year high. That's a problem for the Fed. Yet, under the leadership of new chair Kevin Warsh, it opted not to hike interest rates. So, who are inflation's winners and losers?
item.content:encoded: Inflation is at a three-year high. That's a problem for the Fed. Yet, under the leadership of new chair Kevin Warsh, it opted not to hike interest rates. So, who are inflation's winners and losers?
item.dc:creator: Darian Woods

item.title: Two former governors are teaming up to address potential job losses from AI
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep talks with two former governors, Indiana Republican Eric Holcomb and Rhode Island Democrat Gina Raimondo, about combatting AI-related job losses.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep talks with two former governors, Indiana Republican Eric Holcomb and Rhode Island Democrat Gina Raimondo, about combatting AI-related job losses.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: High gas prices hurt, even if you travel by rubber raft
item.description: River trips require car and truck shuttles to get floaters from the end back to where they started. High gas prices have some cancelling their plans.
item.content:encoded: River trips require car and truck shuttles to get floaters from the end back to where they started. High gas prices have some cancelling their plans.
item.dc:creator: Chris Clements

item.title: When falling housing prices are good news — and when they're not
item.description: Denver renters are celebrating falling housing costs. But sometimes cheaper housing is a sign of economic decline. How can you tell the difference?
item.content:encoded:
Denver renters are celebrating falling housing costs. But sometimes cheaper housing is a sign of economic decline. How can you tell the difference?

(Image credit: Scott Olson)
item.dc:creator: Greg Rosalsky

item.title: Alan Greenspan, the legendary former Federal Reserve chair, dies
item.description: During his chairmanship, Greenspan was celebrated as possibly the best central banker in history. But later, his reputation was tarnished by the worst financial crisis since the Great Depression.
item.content:encoded:
During his chairmanship, Greenspan was celebrated as possibly the best central banker in history. But later, his reputation was tarnished by the worst financial crisis since the Great Depression.

(Image credit: Paul J. Richards)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Fed holds interest rates steady and hints at future rate hike
item.description: The Federal Reserve held its benchmark interest rate steady Wednesday, and hinted its next move could be a rate increase. The new Fed chairman vowed to bring inflation under control.
item.content:encoded: The Federal Reserve held its benchmark interest rate steady Wednesday, and hinted its next move could be a rate increase. The new Fed chairman vowed to bring inflation under control.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Poll: Most Americans have the summer blues about Trump and the economy
item.description: A new NPR/PBS News/Marist poll finds a record low share of Americans approve of President Trump's job performance and his handling of the economy heading into the summer before a key midterm election.
item.content:encoded:
A new NPR/PBS News/Marist poll finds a record low share of Americans approve of President Trump's job performance and his handling of the economy heading into the summer before a key midterm election.

(Image credit: Charlie Neibergall)
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler
